name: octoparse_get_task_data_count
description:
Retrieves the total count of collected data for a specific task. Use this to check how much data has been scraped without downloading all the data.

[When to use]:
- MUST call when user asks "how much data", "how many records", "data count", "how many rows"
- Call when user wants to check data volume before exporting
- Call when user wants to monitor scraping progress (how much data collected so far)
- Call when user wants to verify if task has collected any data
- Call to check if there's new data to export (use includeExported=false)

[When NOT to use]:
- DO NOT call if user wants the actual data (use export_task_scraped_data instead)
- DO NOT call if user wants task execution status (use get_task_execution_status instead)
- DO NOT call if user wants task configuration details (use get_task_detailed_info instead)

[Critical Parameters]:
- taskId (REQUIRED): The task ID to check data count for. User must provide this.
- includeExported (OPTIONAL, default: true): Controls which data to count
  * true = Count ALL data (both exported and not-exported) - Total data collected by task
  * false = Count ONLY not-exported data - New data available for export

[Understanding includeExported]:
When includeExported=true (default):
  - Returns TOTAL count of all data ever collected by this task
  - Use when user asks: "How much data in total?", "Total records", "All data count"

When includeExported=false:
  - Returns count of ONLY new/unexported data
  - Use when user asks: "How much new data?", "Unexported records", "Data ready to export"
  - This is data that hasn't been marked as exported yet

[What You Get]:
- total: Total number of data records matching the filter
- offset: Current offset (always 1 in this tool)
- restTotal: Remaining records after offset
- fileIds: Sample file IDs (only first file, used for verification)

[Self-Correction Scenarios]:

Scenario 1: Task doesn't exist
If API returns error "task not found" or similar:
- Return structured error with taskId
- Suggest: "Verify task ID is correct. Use search_user_task_list to find the correct task ID."
- Mark as recoverable

Scenario 2: Task has no data
If total=0:
- This is NOT an error, just means no data collected yet
- Return normal response: {total: 0, message: "Task has not collected any data yet"}
- If task status is "Running", note: "Task is running but hasn't collected data yet"
- If task status is "Stopped" or "Unexecuted", note: "Task hasn't been run or collected no data"

Scenario 3: Permission denied
If API returns permission error:
- Return structured error
- Suggest: "You may not have permission to access this task. Verify the task belongs to your account."
- Mark as non-recoverable

[Examples]:

Example 1: Check total data count
User: "How much data does task abc123 have?"
-> get_task_data_count(taskId: "abc123", includeExported: true)
Response: "Task abc123 has collected 1,543 total records."

Example 2: Check unexported data count
User: "How much new data is available to export for task xyz789?"
-> get_task_data_count(taskId: "xyz789", includeExported: false)
Response: "Task xyz789 has 234 new records ready to export."

Example 3: Before exporting, check if there's data
User: "Check if my Amazon scraper has any new data"
-> 1. search_user_task_list(keyWord: "Amazon")
-> 2. get_task_data_count(taskId: <found_id>, includeExported: false)
-> If count > 0: "Your Amazon scraper has 523 new records ready to export."
-> If count = 0: "No new data to export. All data has been exported already."

Example 4: Task with no data (self-correction)
User: "How much data in task 456?"
-> get_task_data_count(taskId: "456", includeExported: true)
-> API returns: total=0
Response: "Task 456 has not collected any data yet. Would you like to start the task to begin collecting data?"

Example 5: Invalid task ID (self-correction)
User: "Check data count for task invalid-id"
-> get_task_data_count(taskId: "invalid-id", includeExported: true)
-> API returns: 404 or task not found error
Response: "⚠️ Task 'invalid-id' was not found. Please verify the task ID is correct. You can use search_user_task_list to find your tasks and their IDs."

Example 6: Compare exported vs unexported
User: "How much data total and how much is new for task 789?"
-> 1. get_task_data_count(taskId: "789", includeExported: true) // Get total
-> 2. get_task_data_count(taskId: "789", includeExported: false) // Get unexported
Response: "Task 789 has 2,000 total records, of which 500 are new (not yet exported)."

[Common User Questions and Responses]:

Q: "How many records in my task?"
A: Call with includeExported=true (default) to get total count

Q: "Is there new data to export?"
A: Call with includeExported=false to get unexported count

Q: "How much data has this task collected so far?"
A: Call with includeExported=true to get total count

Q: "Should I export my task data?"
A: Call with includeExported=false first. If count > 0, suggest exporting.

[Integration with Other Tools]:

Before export_task_scraped_data:
-> get_task_data_count (check if there's data to export)
-> If count > 0: proceed with export
-> If count = 0: inform user there's no new data

After start_cloud_task_execution:
-> Wait for task to run for a while
-> get_task_data_count (monitor progress)
-> Show user how much data has been collected

Before clear_task_collected_data:
-> get_task_data_count with includeExported=true
-> Show user how much data will be deleted
-> Ask for confirmation

taskId.description: REQUIRED: The task ID to check data count for. Must be a valid existing task. User must provide this parameter.

includeExported.description: OPTIONAL: Whether to include exported data in the count. Default is true (count all data). Set to false to count only unexported/new data. Use true when user asks about "total data" or "all data", use false when user asks about "new data" or "unexported data".
